title: Perhaps an overated site, hardly any  birds - lots of wildebeest though
text: There's a lot of hype about this place before we came here. It's about an hours drive from Gweta. The name appears misleading as we hardly saw significant numbers of birds. The only ones we saw were sandpiper, ducks and about 5 pelicans far out from the shore.  One pelican made a fleeting appearance near the shoreline but not enough for decent photos to be taken. However,  we saw heaps of wildebeest on the  salt pan. The place is not busy. There are a few raised lookouts near the lake for spotting wildlife. The winds can be pretty strong here with the flat landscape,  hence you may need a jumper. Entry fee is payable. With bird migration,  bird populations here are perhaps seasonal.
original_title: Perhaps an overated site, hardly any  birds - lots of wildebeest though

title: Worth a tour
text: What’s not to like.  An organised open 4wd tour organised through the hotel. Plenty of birds including flamingos, beautiful sunset and lovely scenery. As part of a trip to the Makgadikgadi Pans it’s a no brainer
original_text: What’s not to like.  An organised open 4wd tour organised through the hotel. Plenty of birds including flamingos, beautiful sunset and lovely scenery. As part of a trip to the Makgadikgadi Pans it’s a no brainer

title: Disappointing Nata Bird Sanctuary
text: Really not what I was expecting. Poorly maintained, very little info at the gate and very low numbers of birds. You really have to work to see birds. It’s what you expect of Africa, but all the hype that is written on the net and in brochure raises your expectation way above what you see. A simple map handout, good signage and a blackboard with current sightings would really help with visitor experience and expectations. Unfortunately cannot see that happening.

A simple map handout, good signage and a blackboard with current sightings would really help with visitor experience and expectations. Unfortunately cannot see that happening.

title: Much ado about little
text: The so-called Bird Sanctuary was lacking birds. We saw a total of 6 birds in the park, which is not surprising given the dry weather. The tour through the park was therefore quite uneventful, as was the sundowner. We could have saved ourselves the trouble, as the long journey was definitely not worth it.

The tour through the park was therefore quite uneventful, as was the sundowner. We could have saved ourselves the trouble, as the long journey was definitely not worth it.

title: Nata Bird Sanctuary with Joy was a joy!
text: Our tour of the Nata Bird Sanctuary was arranged through Nata Lodge our guide was named Joy. He was such a good tracker and had lots of experience with birds. Unfortunately they did not get the rains as expected last year so the pans were dry which meant no water birds came when we were there. We did see the wildebeest, ostrich, jackals, rabbits, springbok, zebras and dozens of different birds.   Joy was funny, he was an expert in finding and identifying birds and he even brought tea and biscuits for us too.

title: Wonderful day out
text: This place may divide people, but we absolute loved it. They have done up the viewing hides so they are really very nice. Wildlife will depend on when you go, but in November we saw lots of wildebeest, jackals, and different birds. The horizon was pink with flamingoes and pelicans in the pan. I’m guessing some people might drive in and wonder what all the fuss is about as it’s a flat plain with some wildebeest , but if you take the time and give this community run place a chance it is really very special :)

title: Probably better in the wet season
text: This time of year the birds are few and far between.  We did get lovely shots of a mated pair of Ostrich.  The rosy spoonbills were in action as well.  We could see how great it would be here when it’s wet!
original_title: Probably better in the wet season

title: Brilliant place, great birds, wildlife and landscape.
text:
Always a great visit, but this time (August 2022) the pans were full of water. We've seen a few pelicans and flamingos here before, but this year the pan had hundreds of pelicans and other birds. They were fishing in large "rafts" close to shore. We also had good sightings of Kori Bustard and antelopes.

Don't use the viewing platform, it is no longer safe. But it's not necessary.

Friendly staff at the reception. We used the toilets then drove out to the pans. The roads were dry and OK (Fortuner 4x4, but a normal car is OK when it is dry).

We had excellent lunch at nearby Nata Lodge, also great place to stay.

title: Une belle balade
text:
Pas d'animaux à voir au moment où nous y sommes passé.
Mais c'est quand même sympa de se retrouver seuls au milieu de nulle part et d'apprécier les paysages.
véhicule 4x4 nécessaire

véhicule 4x4 nécessaire
translated_text: No animals to see when we were there. But it's still nice to find yourself alone in the middle of nowhere and appreciate the scenery. 4x4 vehicle necessary

title: Winter visit review!
text: We didn't saw a single bird! No, I am exagerating, there were about 12, including 3 pelicans, but not more than that. I'm sure it's completely different in the wet season, at least, I do hope so.
original_text: We didn't saw a single bird! No, I am exagerating, there were about 12, including 3 pelicans, but not more than that. I'm sure it's completely different in the wet season, at least, I do hope so.

title: It was awful
text:
The sign at the gate clearly says no picnics. I certainly have no issues with a picnic, but we were met at the viewing tower with about 50 people falling around with loud music and bottles of alcohol everywhere. It seems to be a good weekend spot for some of the people living in Nata for an excursion. I would not come over a weekend. It was unpleasant and considering the pans were full of water, it was the only place to get close to the pans. So we basically drove in, saw some Wildebeest on the way in, no birds and a hoard of drunk people hogging the only viewing site.
At the gate the lady did say that there were many Motswana inside, but we should try and find a spot for ourselves. I didn’t know what she meant by the comment until we reached the pans. My family were very disappointed in the experience and from arriving at the gate and leaving it was less than an hour. A waste of time and money on this occasion.

title: Visit the salt pans
text: Took an afternoon self drive with my parents and some friends. Made it to the watch tower very easily and enjoyed a lunch there whilst overlooking the salt pans. They are currently full of water so you cannot go onto them. Saw some pelicans but flamingos are not yet there due to water being too high. Also saw some wildebeest. Lovely day trip and well worth the visit if you’ve never been before. If you don’t want to drive there yourself or do not feel your vehicle will make the dirt road you can book a trip with Nata Lodge and you can go in one of their vehicles
